entries.definition:
bondage of marriage] in an amicable way.
سَراحًا sarāḥan [v. n.] releasing, setting free (33:49) فَمَتِّعُوهُنَّ وَسَرِّحُوهُنَّ سَراحًا جَمِيلا make provision for them and release them in an amicable way.

تَسْرِيح tasrīḥ [v. n. of v. II] setting free, releasing (2:229) الطلاق مرتان فإمساك بمعروف أو تسريح بإحسان divorce is [revocable only] twice, [after which husbands are] either to keep [their wives] in an acceptable manner or release [them] graciously.
entries.definition_english:
bondage of marriage] in an amicable way.
سَراحًا sarāḥan [v. n.] releasing, setting free (33:49) فَمَتِّعُوهُنَّ وَسَرِّحُوهُنَّ سَراحًا جَمِيلا make provision for them and release them in an amicable way.

تَسْرِيح tasrīḥ [v. n. of v. II] setting free, releasing (2:229) الطلاق مرتان فإمساك بمعروف أو تسريح بإحسان divorce is [revocable only] twice, [after which husbands are] either to keep [their wives] in an acceptable manner or release [them] graciously.

entries.definition:
s-r-r the inside, base (e.g. of the head or the navel), innermost part of an object; secrecy, secrets; seat, bed; pleasure, to please, to delight. Of this root, nine forms occur 44 times in the Qur’an: تُسَرّ tasurr once; أَسَرَّ ʾasarra 18 times; إِسْرار ʾisrār twice; سِرّ sirr 11 times; سُرور surūr once; مَسْرور masrūr twice; سَرائِر sarāʾir once; السَّرّاء sarrāʾ twice and سُرُر surur six times.
تُسَرّ tasurr [imperf. of v. II, trans.] to delight, to please, to make happy (2:69) إِنَّها بَقَرَةٌ صَفْراءُ فاقِعٌ لَوْنُها تَسُرُّ النَّاظِرِينَ it should be a bright yellow cow, that delights the onlookers.

432
entries.definition_english:
s-r-r the inside, base (e.g. of the head or the navel), innermost part of an object; secrecy, secrets; seat, bed; pleasure, to please, to delight. Of this root, nine forms occur 44 times in the Qur’an: تُسَرّ tasurr once; أَسَرَّ ʾasarra 18 times; إِسْرار ʾisrār twice; سِرّ sirr 11 times; سُرور surūr once; مَسْرور masrūr twice; سَرائِر sarāʾir once; السَّرّاء sarrāʾ twice and سُرُر surur six times.
تُسَرّ tasurr [imperf. of v. II, trans.] to delight, to please, to make happy (2:69) إِنَّها بَقَرَةٌ صَفْراءُ فاقِعٌ لَوْنُها تَسُرُّ النَّاظِرِينَ it should be a bright yellow cow, that delights the onlookers.

432

entries.definition:
أَسَرَّ ʾasarra [v. IV, trans.] 1 to conceal, to keep as a secret (12:77) فَأَسَرَّها يُوسُفُ فِي نَفْسِهِ وَلَمْ يُبْدِها لَهُمْ but Joseph kept it [secret] to himself and did not reveal it to them 2 to convey in secrecy, to tell in confidence (66:3) وَإِذْ أَسَرَّ النَّبِيُّ إِلى بَعْضِ أَزْواجِهِ حَدِيثًا and when the Prophet told something in confidence to one of his wives; *(20:62) فَأَسَرُّوا النَّجْوَى so they conferred secretly 3 to treasure, to hide (12:19) يا بُشْرى هذَا غُلامٌ وَأَسَرُّوهُ بِضاعَةً ‘Good news!’ Here is a boy!, and they treasured him as merchandise 4 (possibly, according to an opposite meaning of سرّ asarr) to reveal, to declare, to manifest (10:54) وَأَسَرُّوا النَّدامَةَ لَمَّا رَأَوُا الْعَذابَ and they will be openly remorseful when they see the chastisement (or, according to the more popular interpretation, secretly).
إِسْرار ʾisrār [v. n.] 1 concealing, conning, scheming (47:26) ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قالُوا لِلَّذِينَ كَرِهُوا ما نَزَّلَ اللَّهُ سَنُطِيعُكُمْ فِي بَعْضِ الْأَمْرِ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ إِسْرارَهُمْ that was so because they said to those who hate what God has sent down, ‘We will obey you in some matters’, but God knows their secret scheming 2 [adverbially] confidentially, secretly, privately (71:9) ثُمَّ إِنِّي أَعْلَنْتُ لَهُمْ وَأَسْرَرْتُ لَهُمْ إِسْرارًا then I have tried preaching to them in public and speaking to them in private.

سِرّ sirr I [n.; pl. أَسْرار ʾasrār] 1 secret (43:80) أَمْ يَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّا لا نَسْمَعُ سِرَّهُمْ وَنَجْواهُمْ do they think We cannot hear their secret talk and their private counsel? 2 mystery (25:6) قُلْ أَنْزَلَهُ الَّذِي يَعْلَمُ السِّرَّ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ say ‘It was sent down by Him who knows the secrets/mystery of the heavens and earth’ II [adverb.] in private, secretly, furtively (2:235) وَلكِنْ لا تُواعِدُوهُنَّ سِرًّا but do not make agreements with them secretly.

سُرور surūr [n.] pleasure, happiness (76:11) وَلَقَّاهُمْ نَضْرَةً وَسُرورًا and He accorded them radiance and gladness.

مَسْرور masrūr [pass. part.] well-pleased, rejoicing (84:9) وَيَنْقَلِبُ إِلى أَهْلِهِ مَسْرورًا and return to his people well-pleased.

السرّاء ʾal-sarrāʾ [n.] (in opposition to الضَّرّاء ʾal-ḍarrāʾ, q.v.) happy state of life, freedom from adversity, happiness (3:134) الَّذِينَ يُنْفِقُونَ فِي السَّرّاءِ وَالضَّرّاءِ those who give both in prosperity and adversity; *(7:95) وَقالُوا قَدْ مَسَّ آباءَنَا الضَّرّاءُ وَالسَّرّاءُ and they said, ‘Hardship and affluence befell our forefathers’ (or, they had a
